meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no marketing skills (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no marketing skills. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Learn to bridge the gap between building and growth. This intermediate case study shows how to implement analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing why users bounce. By implementing a robust event-tracking analytics stack, you transform raw data into actionable product decisions. This setup allows you to identify exactly where users drop off, enabling data-driven improvements that replace the need for expensive marketing campaigns with high-converting, user-centric product iterations.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks perfecting your codebase, only to launch to the sound of silence. The 'build it and they will come' myth is the silent killer of student projects. Without a marketing budget or a following, you aren't fighting for traffic—you are fighting for data. 

True engineering isn't just about syntax; it's about understanding the human behavior behind the clicks. By instrumenting your app with granular analytics, you turn your project into a laboratory. You stop being a developer and start being a product scientist, using quantitative feedback to optimize your funnel and prove your value to future employers.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single user action that defines value in your app (e.g., 'Account Created' or 'Data Exported').
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and suggest a schema for tracking 'North Star' events. Focus on user acquisition and retention milestones.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Review my database schema and suggest a table structure for capturing event-driven analytics.'

steps.title: Instrumenting the Event Pipeline
steps.description: Create a unified event emitter that captures user metadata and context without cluttering your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lightweight wrapper for event tracking that handles asynchronous logging to an analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create an analytics.ts service that exports a trackEvent(name, properties) function.'

steps.title: Mapping the User Journey
steps.description: Inject tracking hooks into your core UI components to capture clicks, form submissions, and navigation patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the key interactive components in this codebase and suggest where to place tracking hooks for maximum conversion visibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Identify all form submission handlers and suggest adding trackEvent calls to each.'

steps.title: Implementing Session Replay
steps.description: Visual data is better than spreadsheets. Set up session recording to see exactly where users get confused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide instructions for integrating a privacy-focused session replay SDK into a React/Next.js environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Install the session-replay package and wrap the root layout with the provider.'

steps.title: Automating Dashboard Alerts
steps.description: Set up automated notifications for when your key metrics drop, so you can iterate on the problem immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to aggregate daily event logs and send a summary report to a Slack webhook.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a cron job script that parses the analytics database and sends a daily summary to a webhook.'

pro_tips: Never track 'vanity metrics' like page views; focus exclusively on conversion events that correlate with user success.
Always sanitize your analytics data; never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) to third-party tracking services.
Implement 'feature flags' alongside analytics so you can turn off tracking for specific users without a full redeploy.
Use 'Cohort Analysis' to compare how users who signed up in Week 1 behave differently than those in Week 2.
If your app has a low user count, prioritize 'Session Replay' over 'Event Aggregation' to gain qualitative insights faster.
content.next_steps: Deploy your updated build to a staging environment, simulate 10 user sessions, and verify the events are appearing in your dashboard before pushing to production.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to prove your app's value through data, not just lines of code. This is the exact skill set that separates senior engineers from junior developers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
